statement.markdown: Given a sorted array `arr` of integers, write a function to find the index of the target element using binary search. If the target is not found, return -1. However, the function has a subtle bug that causes it to incorrectly handle edge cases. The function should return the index of the first occurrence of the target element.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a sorted array of integers `arr` and the target element `target`.
statement.outputFormat: The function should return the index of the first occurrence of the target element if found, otherwise return -1.
statement.notes: The array will contain at least one element, and the target element may or may not be present in the array.

examples.explanation: The first occurrence of 3 is at index 2.
The target element 7 is not found in the array.

hints.text: Review the binary search algorithm and its edge cases.
Check the condition for returning the index of the target element.

editorial.title: Binary Search Function
editorial.intuition: The binary search algorithm works by repeatedly dividing the search interval in half. If the value of the search key is less than the item in the middle of the interval, narrow the interval to the lower half. Otherwise, narrow it to the upper half. Repeatedly check until the value is found or the interval is empty.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach would be to iterate through the array from the start to the end, checking each element against the target. However, this approach has a time complexity of O(n) and is not efficient for large arrays.
editorial.betterSolution: The binary search approach has a time complexity of O(log n) and is more efficient for large arrays.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to implement the binary search algorithm correctly, handling all edge cases.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The binary search algorithm is correct because it repeatedly divides the search interval in half, ensuring that the target element is found if it exists in the array.
editorial.dryRun: To dry run the binary search algorithm, start with the entire array and repeatedly divide it in half until the target element is found or the interval is empty.
commonMistakes: Not returning the index of the first occurrence of the target element

functionSignature.python: def binary_search(self, arr, target: int) -> int:
